steps.title: Use the rewrite trick for $f(1)$
steps.content: An exponential $f(x) = a \cdot b^{x-1}$ has $f(1) = a \cdot b^{0} = a$. So if the exponent has the form $x - 1$, the coefficient $a$ is $f(1) = k$.

steps.title: Pick the form with exponent $x-1$
steps.content:
Each choice represents the same exponential, just rewritten. Choice C has the form $f(x) = 144.5(1.7)^{x-1}$, so:
$$f(1) = 144.5(1.7)^{0} = 144.5$$
The coefficient $144.5$ is $k = f(1)$ — exactly what the question asks for. That matches choice C — C is correct.
